content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

The implementation correctly addresses all three issues from the project spec. Issue 1 (struct drift): eliminated by consolidating to a single canonical struct in model.rs with round-trip tests. Issue 2 (runless-to-named completion): correctly implemented with started_at-gated matching and comprehensive guard arms. Issue 3 (plan_hash auto-population): correctly auto-populates from snapshot only on new entry creation, intentionally avoiding existing-row backfill to prevent mislabeling after plan evolution. All 353 tests pass, 13 new tests provide thorough coverage of the happy paths and key edge cases (plan evolution replays, started_at mismatches, legacy row preservation). The code is clean with well-placed comments explaining the invariants. Two minor amendments proposed: (1) json_fill_none silently swallows serde errors, and (2) deny_unknown_fields has a forward-compatibility tradeoff on rollback that should be documented.

## Amendments

1. The `json_fill_none` function (fs.rs:2284-2300) silently converts serialization/deserialization errors into `None`, which is indistinguishable from a structural merge rejection. In the `StartJournalDetails::merge` and `CompletionJournalDetails::merge` callers, this means a serde bug (e.g., a future field type that fails to round-trip through `serde_json::Value`) would appear as a rejected merge, causing duplicate journal events to be appended instead of being merged. Consider returning `Result<Option<T>, ...>` or at minimum adding a `tracing::warn` inside `json_fill_none` when `to_value` or `from_value` fails, so operators can distinguish serde failures from legitimate merge rejections.
   Rationale: Silent error swallowing in a repair path can mask latent bugs. Today the types are simple (String, Option<String>, DateTime) so this won't fire, but the function is generic and future-facing — its whole purpose is to be forward-compatible with new fields. A new field with a non-trivial serde implementation could silently break journal repair without any diagnostic signal.
2. `#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]` on `StartJournalDetails` and `CompletionJournalDetails` (model.rs:357, 383) prevents parsing journal entries that contain fields added by a newer code version. If the binary is rolled back after new-format entries are written, `parse()` returns `None`, journal merge is skipped, and duplicate events accumulate. This is data-safe but operationally surprising. Add a comment documenting this forward-compatibility tradeoff next to the `deny_unknown_fields` attribute, or switch to a lenient parse for existing journal entries (only use `deny_unknown_fields` in the round-trip test assertions where it actually catches drift).
   Rationale: The struct consolidation was done specifically to prevent drift, and `deny_unknown_fields` is one of the tools used. But there's a tension: `deny_unknown_fields` prevents drift on parse but also prevents forward compatibility on rollback. Since the round-trip tests already catch drift at compile time (the primary goal), `deny_unknown_fields` at runtime is belt-and-suspenders with a downside. At minimum, the tradeoff should be documented so a future developer doesn't spend time debugging why journal repair stopped working after a rollback.
